report_name: GAO Report: DHS Cybersecurity Modernization Programs Face Cost, Staffing, and Threat Evolution Challenges

identities.name: U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO)
identities.description: The U.S. government oversight body that published the report (GAO-26-107983) assessing the Department of Homeland Security's cybersecurity acquisition programs.

identities.name: Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
identities.description: The U.S. federal department whose major cybersecurity acquisition programs were the subject of the GAO report. The report found these programs face challenges in cost, staffing, and keeping pace with evolving threats.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
identities.description: The operational arm of DHS responsible for managing key cybersecurity initiatives like the Continuous Diagnostics and Mitigation (CDM) program, as reviewed in the GAO report.

identities.name: Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies
identities.description: The group of federal agencies that rely on DHS and CISA programs for threat detection, visibility, and incident response. The effectiveness of these programs directly impacts their cybersecurity posture.

identities.name: Continuous Diagnostics and Mitigation (CDM)
identities.description: A key cybersecurity program managed by CISA, designed to provide threat detection and visibility for federal agencies. The GAO report noted it is on track to meet 2026 goals based on revised definitions.

identities.name: CyberSentry
identities.description: A DHS cybersecurity program that extends protection to U.S. critical infrastructure. The GAO report's findings on program challenges have national security implications for this area.
